Pitch: A courageous flock of migrating geese must transport a cursed breadcrumb to Mount Doom, but they quickly realize the dark lord Sauron has deployed a massive anti-avian net over his realm. 2. Phonetic Target: 'The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring'. Key phonetic targets: Lord (-ord), Rings (-ings), Fellowship (-el-o-ship), Ring (-ing). 3. Thematic Brainstorm: Flock -> birds, fly, feathers, beak, wings, geese, nest, migration. 4. Intersection: 'Wings' rhymes perfectly with 'Rings' (and 'Wing' with 'Ring'), yielding 'The Lord of the Wings'.

text: One does not simply Talk into Mordor.

Thematic Brainstorm: talk, shout, yell, speak, bellow, phone, call, chatter. 4. Intersection: 'Bellow' (loud talking) rhymes perfectly with 'Fellow' in 'Fellowship'. Parody Title = 'The Lord of the Rings: The Bellow-ship of the Ring'.

text: Ring Kong ain't got shit on me!

Intersection: 'Ringing' perfectly mirrors the syllable structure and '-ing' suffix of 'Training', while directly incorporating the pun word. Parody Title = 'Ringing Day'.

text: Spring Kong ain't got shit on me!

Intersection: 'Raining' perfectly rhymes with 'Training' and beautifully ties into the 'April showers bring May flowers' motif of spring. Parody Title = 'Raining Day'.

text: She doesn't even Glow here!

Intersection: 'Green' perfectly rhymes with 'Mean' and captures the classic cartoonish hue of radioactive glowing. Parody Title = 'Green Girls'.

The Intersection: 'Whirls' perfectly rhymes with 'Girls' and captures the spinning, dizzying sensation of Vertigo. Parody Title = 'Mean Whirls'.

text: Three doesn't even go here!
parody_pitch: A naive numerator transfers to a school ruled by a toxic clique of fractions, only to spark absolute mathematical chaos when the prime number Three tries to sit at their lunch table.

Pitch: A naive numerator transfers to a high school ruled by an exclusive clique of fractions, and discovers the brutal social hierarchy when a prime number tries to sit at their table. 2. Phonetic Target: 'Mean Girls'. 'Mean' rhymes with teen, screen, lean. 'Girls' has an 'erlz' sound, rhyming with pearls, curls, and swirls. 3. Brainstorm: Words related to 'Three', division, and math: odd, prime, fraction, math, thirds, sum, denominator. 4. Intersection: 'Thirds' shares the same 'ir' vowel sound and plural 's' ending as 'Girls', making it a great phonetic slant match. Bonus: 'Mean' is already a mathematical term (average)! text: Hairy nice!

thought_process: 1. The Intersection: 'Grow' (as in growing hair) rhymes perfectly with 'Bo', and 'mat' (as in a tangled mat of hair) rhymes perfectly with 'rat'. Pitch: The Rebel Alliance is lured into a plush, overly-cushioned Death Star equipped with soothing white noise machines, prompting a sleepy Admiral Ackbar to realize they haven't been ambushed, they've just been tucked in. 2. text: Yeah, Maybe!
